name: Carbon dioxide partial pressure in arterial blood

concept.conceptName: Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Arterial blood
Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Arterial blood

en.name: Carbon dioxide partial pressure in arterial blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The arterial partial pressure of carbon dioxide (PaCO2) is the pressure exerted by CO2 dissolved in arterial blood. It reflects alveolar ventilation: PaCO2 rises when ventilation is insufficient (hypoventilation, respiratory failure) and falls when ventilation is excessive (hyperventilation) [1]. Together with pH and bicarbonate, it characterises the respiratory component of acid-base balance. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a blood gas analyser on an arterial sample. Results are reported as a partial pressure in mmHg (US convention) or kPa (SI / European convention) [2]. **Note: arterial vs venous.** Arterial and venous PCO2 differ substantially (venous PCO2 is typically 4-6 mmHg higher). The arterio-venous PCO2 gradient is itself a clinical signal of perfusion. This concept set covers **arterial** PCO2 only. Venous and central venous PCO2 are captured by the separate concept set "Carbon dioxide partial pressure in venous blood". **Note: temperature-corrected variant.** Modern blood gas analysers measure at 37 °C. A separate LOINC code exists for PCO2 corrected to the patient's actual temperature (relevant in hypothermia, e.g. therapeutic hypothermia post-cardiac arrest). Both forms are included here; downstream analyses that need to distinguish them must use the LOINC code, not the value. **Note: do not confuse with total CO2.** LOINC distinguishes `Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure]` (PaCO2, dissolved gas, mmHg or kPa) from `Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume]` (total CO2 content of the sample, mmol/L) — the latter is essentially the bicarbonate plus dissolved CO2 and belongs with the separate concept set "Bicarbonate in blood". Only `[Partial pressure]` codes are included here. ## Included Concepts The set lists two LOINC "Lab Test" concepts for PCO2 in arterial blood (cord blood excluded): - *3027946 / 2019-8 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *3045156 / 32771-8 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: temperature-corrected variant. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mm[Hg]`** (concept 8876 — millimetre of mercury). Conversion: `1 mmHg = 0.1333 kPa`, `1 kPa ≈ 7.50 mmHg`. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts that exist for CO2 in arterial blood are deliberately not part of this set, grouped by reason for exclusion. ### Total CO2 — different measurand - *3009609 / 2026-3 — Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood* - *3037663 / 41647-9 — Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood by calculation* ### Cord blood specimens — physiologically distinct - *3003932 / 28644-3 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Arterial cord blood* - *3032759 / 49694-3 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Arterial cord blood* - *3043950 / 39466-8 — Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Arterial cord blood* - *40761030 / 57921-9 — Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Arterial cord blood by calculation* ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports an arterial PCO2 (e.g. blood gas analyser): `3027946 / 2019-8 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Arterial blood`. - **Source unit is kPa**: convert to mmHg using `1 kPa ≈ 7.50 mmHg`. - **Source reports PCO2 from venous, mixed venous or central venous blood**: do **not** map to this concept set. Use "Carbon dioxide partial pressure in venous blood" instead. - **Source reports "total CO2" or "TCO2" (mmol/L)**: do **not** map. Map to the "Bicarbonate in blood" concept set (clinically equivalent in routine practice). - **Do not confuse with**: venous PCO2 (separate concept set), bicarbonate (separate concept set), end-tidal CO2 (EtCO2, exhaled gas — different measurand). ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2019-8 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Arterial blood. Available: https://loinc.org/2019-8/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
